canonical_question: What are the core dynamic programming patterns and techniques?
aliases: dynamic programming patterns for coding interviews
DP memoization vs tabulation when to use
knapsack LCS LIS coin change DP solutions
bitmask DP digit DP tree DP interval DP guide
how to identify and solve DP problems
constraints: DP requires both overlapping subproblems AND optimal substructure; verify both before choosing DP
Bitmask DP limited to n <= 20-25 elements due to 2^n state space
Memoized recursion risks stack overflow for deep recursion (n > 10,000 in most languages)
Space-optimized DP (rolling array) destroys solution reconstruction capability
Digit DP requires digit-by-digit decomposition; iterating entire range is infeasible for large bounds

skip_this_unit_if.condition: Problem has greedy choice property and no overlapping subproblems
skip_this_unit_if.use_instead: Use greedy algorithms (activity selection, Huffman coding, Kruskal/Prim)

skip_this_unit_if.condition: Problem requires exploring all valid configurations without optimization
skip_this_unit_if.use_instead: Use backtracking with pruning (N-Queens, Sudoku, permutation generation)

skip_this_unit_if.condition: State space is a graph with unweighted edges
skip_this_unit_if.use_instead: Use BFS/DFS for graph traversal; DP on DAGs only when topological order exists

inputs_needed.question: What type of problem are you solving?
Which DP pattern best matches your problem structure?
